segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In today's astronomy lecture, I'm going to talk about the need for a system to manage the movement of satellites and other objects in orbit around the Earth.
segments.zh: 在今天的天文课上，我要讲的是建立一套系统来管理卫星和地球轨道上其他物体运行的必要性，也就是空间交通管理系统。我们已经有了有效的空中交通管制系统，在国际上用于确保飞机安全飞行。空间交通管理是类似的概念，但重点是控制卫星。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In other words, a Space Traffic Management system.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: We already have effective Air Traffic Control systems that are used internationally to ensure that planes navigate our skies safely.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Well, Space Traffic Management is a similar concept, but focusing on the control of satellites.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The aim of such a system would be to prevent the danger of collisions in space between the objects in orbit around the Earth.
segments.zh: 这样一个系统的目标是防止地球轨道上的物体在太空中发生碰撞的危险。为此，我们需要一套法律措施，还必须开发技术系统来防止这类事故。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In order to do this, we'd need to have a set of legal measures and we'd also have to develop the technical systems to enable us to prevent such accidents.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But unfortunately, at present we don't actually have a Space Traffic Management system that works.
segments.zh: 但不幸的是，目前我们实际上没有一个有效的空间交通管理系统。那么为什么没有呢？开发这样一个系统有什么问题？

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So why not?

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: What are the problems in developing such a system?

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Well, for one thing, satellites are relatively cheap these days, compared with how they were in the past, meaning that more people can afford to put them into space.
segments.zh: 首先，与过去相比，现在的卫星相对便宜，这意味着更多的人有能力将卫星送入太空。所以太空中的卫星数量大大增加，而且人们不仅发射单个卫星，还发射整个卫星星座，由成千上万颗协同工作的卫星组成。因此，太空变得越来越拥挤。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So there's a lot more of them out there, and people aren't just launching single satellites but whole constellations, consisting of thousands of them designed to work together.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So space is getting more crowded every day.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But in spite of this, one thing you may be surprised to learn is that you can launch a satellite into space and, once it's out there, it doesn't have to send back any information to Earth to allow its identification.
segments.zh: 但尽管如此，有一件事可能会让你惊讶：你可以将一颗卫星发射到太空，一旦它在那里，它不必向地球发回任何信息来识别身份。所以，虽然我们有国际系统来确保我们知道天空中的飞机在哪里，并防止它们相撞，但就卫星安全而言，目前我们根本没有足够的跟踪方法。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So while we have international systems for ensuring we know where the planes in our skies are, and to prevent them from colliding with one another, when it comes to the safety of satellites, at present we don't have anything like enough proper ways of tracking them.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: And it isn't just entire satellites that we need to consider.
segments.zh: 而且我们不仅需要考虑完整的卫星。更大的威胁是地球轨道上大量的太空碎片——卫星的碎片、空间站的垃圾等等。其中一些非常小，很难识别，但仍然非常危险。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: A greater threat is the huge amount of space debris in orbit around the Earth – broken bits of satellite and junk from space stations and so on.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: And some of these are so small that they can be very hard to identify, but they can still be very dangerous.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In addition, some operators may be unwilling to share information about the satellites they've launched.
segments.zh: 此外，一些运营商可能不愿意分享他们发射的卫星的信息。例如，一颗卫星可能是为军事目的设计的，或者可能是出于商业原因发射的，运营商不希望竞争对手获得相关信息。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: For example, a satellite may be designed for military purposes, or it may have been launched for commercial reasons, and the operators don't want competitors to have information about it.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: And even if the operators are willing to provide it, the information isn't easy to collect.
segments.zh: 即使运营商愿意提供信息，这些信息也不容易收集。我们需要关于物体本身的详细信息，以及它在特定时间的位置——记住，卫星不是很大，而且可能以每小时数千公里的速度移动。我们没有传感器能够持续跟踪如此快速移动的物体，所以科学家们所能做的就是预测卫星下一步将飞向哪里。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Details are needed about the object itself, as well as about its location at a particular time – and remember that a satellite isn't very big, and it's likely to be moving at thousands of kilometres an hour.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: We don't have any sensors that can constantly follow something moving so fast, so all that the scientists can do is to put forward a prediction concerning where the satellite is heading next.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So those are some of the problems that we're facing.
segments.zh: 以上就是我们面临的一些问题。现在来看看一些已经提出的解决方案。一个关键问题是如何处理信息。我们需要更多信息，但这些信息也必须在全球范围内可获取，因此我们需要建立大家都能接受的共享标准，来规范信息的呈现方式。我们在其他科学领域已经这样做了，所以尽管这是一个挑战，但并非不可能完成的任务。然后，随着所有信息的收集，需要将它们整合起来以便使用，这就涉及创建一个可以录入信息的单一数据库。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Let's consider now some of the solutions that have been suggested.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: One key issue is the way in which information is dealt with.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: We need more information, but it also needs to be accessible at a global level, so we need to establish shared standards that we can all agree on for the way in which this information is presented.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: We already do this in other areas of science, so although this is a challenge, it's not an impossible task.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Then, as all this information's collected, it needs to be put together so it can be used, and that will involve creating a single database on which it can be entered.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: As we continue to push forward new developments, congestion of the space environment is only going to increase.
segments.zh: 随着我们不断推进新的发展，太空环境的拥堵只会加剧。为了应对这一情况，我们需要开发一个像我刚才描述的系统来协调众多航天器操作员的工作，但同样重要的是，这个系统必须能够建立使用者的信任，无论是在国家层面还是全球层面。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: To cope with this, we need to develop a system like the one I've described to coordinate the work of the numerous spacecraft operators, but it's also essential that this system is one that establishes trust in the people that use it, both nationally and at a global level.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: One interesting development ...
